identity.tagline: A walled medieval town on an island — said to be the birthplace of Marco Polo.

practical.day_trip_note: Reachable as a day trip from Split or Dubrovnik but the old town deserves an evening — the crowds thin out after the day-trip ferries leave.

getting_there.ferry_notes: Multiple connections from Split (via catamaran to Korčula town, or car ferry to Vela Luka on the western tip). A short passenger ferry runs from Orebić on the Pelješac peninsula directly to Korčula town — useful if driving from Dubrovnik.

ferry_routes.community_note: Car ferry to Vela Luka on the western end of the island. You'll need to drive or take a bus to Korčula town from there (~45 min).
Short passenger ferry from the Pelješac peninsula directly to Korčula town. The most convenient connection if coming from Dubrovnik by car — park in Orebić, walk on.

nearby_islands.name: Badija
nearby_islands.description: A small island just off Korčula town with a Franciscan monastery and a deer park. No cars.
nearby_islands.worth_visiting_for: A peaceful half-day walk from Korčula town.

nearby_islands.name: Mljet
nearby_islands.description: A heavily forested island with a national park containing two saltwater lakes. One of Croatia's most beautiful islands.
nearby_islands.worth_visiting_for: The national park — swimming in the lakes, kayaking, cycling the island.

nearby_islands.name: Lastovo
nearby_islands.description: One of Croatia's most remote and least visited islands. A nature park with almost no mass tourism.
nearby_islands.worth_visiting_for: Complete escape. Strong local identity and good seafood.

hidden_gems.title: Pošip and Grk wine
hidden_gems.description: Korčula produces two indigenous white wines — Pošip and Grk — that are genuinely excellent and rarely found outside Croatia. Ask for them by name in any konoba.

hidden_gems.title: Moreška sword dance
hidden_gems.description: A traditional sword dance performed only in Korčula town, with roots going back centuries. Performed regularly in summer for tourists but it is an authentic local tradition, not a fabricated one.

local_warnings: Korčula town gets very busy with cruise ship day-trippers in July and August — mornings are crowded, evenings are peaceful once the ships leave.
Vela Luka (the car ferry port) is a separate town from Korčula town — factor in the transfer time when planning connections.
